creator: ports@toco-domains.de
text:
I can confirm that the build stalls while waiting for input. Taking a look at the buildlog reveals:

=== Start ===

===>   p5-Mail-Mbox-MessageParser-1.5105 depends on package: perl5>=5.18<5.19 - found
===>  Configuring for p5-Mail-Mbox-MessageParser-1.5105
Subroutine auto_tester redefined at inc/Module/Install/AutomatedTester.pm line 10.
Subroutine cpan_tester redefined at inc/Module/Install/AutomatedTester.pm line 15.
*************************************************************************** 
NOTE: The optional module Benchmark::Timer (version 0.7100) is not installed.

Install Benchmark::Timer if you want to run "make testspeed"
Enter the full path, or "none" for none.
Where can I find your "bzip" executable? (bzip2 type) [/usr/bin/bzip2] 

=== End ===

Parv wrote at the beginning:
> Given the options to portmaster, 
> there should not be a need for manual intervention in this case.

But after carefully reading of the man-page this is not true. The documentation claims:
 --no-confirm
         do not ask the user to confirm the list of ports to be installed
         and/or updated before proceeding

It works like intended. There is no confirmation needed for the ports to be installed/updated. There are proceeded without input.

But the given port is a special case. It needs input from the user. And having a look at the type of input needed: this is nothing portmaster can just blindly give.

So: i close this PR, because everything works like intended.

If you don't share my opinion please reopen the ticket and provide a specific answer which input should be used in such cases. Then we will discuss the issue further.
